comment_text.sn35.243:1.2: The completion of a town hall was celebrated by a talk for the Sakyans at MN 53:2.1 and the Mallas at DN 33:1.2.1. Such halls were community meeting places that played a central role in civic society and communal decision-making in democratic republics such as the Mallas and the Sakyans. The Buddha’s participation is a sign of his support for their civic and democratic process; he is not said to attend any comparable civic events for kings.
comment_text.sn35.243:2.8: The Buddha refers to the Sakyans as “Gotamas” after the lineage of the family priest (purohita). Thus “Gotama” is not a personal name of the Buddha. In a similar context at DN 33:1.4.5, the Buddha refers to the Mallas after their priestly lineage, Vāseṭṭha.
comment_text.sn35.243:3.4: The Buddha’s bad back is mentioned several times in similar contexts, suggesting that such events took place in his old age. It serves as a reminder that freedom from attachment to the body does not mean one must forcefully endure pain.
comment_text.sn35.243:3.12: “Festering” is avassuta, literally “that which has been flowed down into”.

root_text.sn35.243:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme.
root_text.sn35.243:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena kāpilavatthavānaṁ sakyānaṁ navaṁ santhāgāraṁ acirakāritaṁ hoti anajjhāvuṭṭhaṁ samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā kenaci vā manussabhūtena.
root_text.sn35.243:1.3: Atha kho kāpilavatthavā sakyā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho kāpilavatthavā sakyā bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn35.243:1.4: “idha, bhante, kāpilavatthavānaṁ sakyānaṁ navaṁ santhāgāraṁ acirakāritaṁ anajjhāvuṭṭhaṁ samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā kenaci vā manussabhūtena.
root_text.sn35.243:1.6: Bhagavatā paṭhamaṁ paribhuttaṁ pacchā kāpilavatthavā sakyā paribhuñjissanti.
root_text.sn35.243:1.7: Tadassa kāpilavatthavānaṁ sakyānaṁ dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāyā”ti.
root_text.sn35.243:2.1: Atha kho kāpilavatthavā sakyā bhagavato adhivāsanaṁ viditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā yena navaṁ santhāgāraṁ tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā sabbasanthariṁ santhāgāraṁ santharitvā āsanāni paññāpetvā udakamaṇikaṁ patiṭṭhāpetvā telappadīpaṁ āropetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn35.243:2.2: “sabbasantharisanthataṁ, bhante, santhāgāraṁ, āsanāni paññattāni, udakamaṇiko patiṭṭhāpito, telappadīpo āropito.
root_text.sn35.243:2.4: Atha kho bhagavā nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya saddhiṁ bhikkhusaṅghena yena navaṁ santhāgāraṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā pāde pakkhāletvā santhāgāraṁ pavisitvā majjhimaṁ thambhaṁ nissāya puratthābhimukho nisīdi.
root_text.sn35.243:2.5: Bhikkhusaṅghopi kho pāde pakkhāletvā santhāgāraṁ pavisitvā pacchimaṁ bhittiṁ nissāya puratthābhimukho nisīdi bhagavantaṁyeva purakkhatvā.
root_text.sn35.243:2.6: Kāpilavatthavā sakyā pāde pakkhāletvā santhāgāraṁ pavisitvā puratthimaṁ bhittiṁ nissāya pacchimābhimukhā nisīdiṁsu bhagavantaṁyeva purakkhatvā.
root_text.sn35.243:2.7: Atha kho bhagavā kāpilavatthave sakye bahudeva rattiṁ dhammiyā kathāya sandassetvā samādapetvā samuttejetvā sampahaṁsetvā uyyojesi:
root_text.sn35.243:2.10: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho kāpilavatthavā sakyā bhagavato paṭissutvā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkamiṁsu.
root_text.sn35.243:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā acirapakkantesu kāpilavatthavesu sakyesu āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn35.243:3.3: Paṭibhātu taṁ, moggallāna, bhikkhūnaṁ dhammī kathā.
root_text.sn35.243:3.6: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno bhagavato paccassosi.
root_text.sn35.243:3.7: Atha kho bhagavā catugguṇaṁ saṅghāṭiṁ paññapetvā dakkhiṇena passena sīhaseyyaṁ kappesi, pāde pādaṁ accādhāya, sato sampajāno uṭṭhānasaññaṁ manasi karitvā.
root_text.sn35.243:3.8: Tatra kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn35.243:3.10: “Āvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato mahāmoggallānassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.sn35.243:3.13: Taṁ suṇātha, sādhukaṁ manasi karotha, bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.sn35.243:3.14: “Evamāvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato mahāmoggallānassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.sn35.243:4.2: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā piyarūpe rūpe adhimuccati, appiyarūpe rūpe byāpajjati, anupaṭṭhitakāyassatī viharati parittacetaso,
root_text.sn35.243:4.3: tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti yatthassa te uppannā pāpakā akusalā dhammā aparisesā nirujjhanti …pe…
root_text.sn35.243:4.5: manasā dhammaṁ viññāya piyarūpe dhamme adhimuccati, appiyarūpe dhamme byāpajjati, anupaṭṭhitakāyassatī ca viharati parittacetaso,
root_text.sn35.243:4.6: tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti yatthassa te uppannā pāpakā akusalā dhammā aparisesā nirujjhanti.
root_text.sn35.243:4.7: Ayaṁ vuccati, āvuso, bhikkhu avassuto cakkhuviññeyyesu rūpesu …pe…
root_text.sn35.243:4.10: Evaṁvihāriñcāvuso, bhikkhuṁ cakkhuto cepi naṁ māro upasaṅkamati labhateva māro otāraṁ, labhati māro ārammaṇaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.243:4.11: jivhāto cepi naṁ māro upasaṅkamati, labhateva māro otāraṁ, labhati māro ārammaṇaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.243:4.12: manato cepi naṁ māro upasaṅkamati, labhateva māro otāraṁ, labhati māro ārammaṇaṁ.
root_text.sn35.243:5.1: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, naḷāgāraṁ vā tiṇāgāraṁ vā sukkhaṁ kolāpaṁ terovassikaṁ.
root_text.sn35.243:5.2: Puratthimāya cepi naṁ disāya puriso ādittāya tiṇukkāya upasaṅkameyya, labhetheva aggi otāraṁ, labhetha aggi ārammaṇaṁ;
root_text.sn35.243:5.3: pacchimāya cepi naṁ disāya puriso ādittāya tiṇukkāya upasaṅkameyya …pe…
root_text.sn35.243:5.8: yato kutoci cepi naṁ puriso ādittāya tiṇukkāya upasaṅkameyya, labhetheva aggi otāraṁ labhetha aggi ārammaṇaṁ.
root_text.sn35.243:5.9: Evameva kho, āvuso, evaṁvihāriṁ bhikkhuṁ cakkhuto cepi naṁ māro upasaṅkamati, labhateva māro otāraṁ, labhati māro ārammaṇaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.243:5.11: manato cepi naṁ māro upasaṅkamati, labhateva māro otāraṁ, labhati māro ārammaṇaṁ.
root_text.sn35.243:5.12: Evaṁvihāriñcāvuso, bhikkhuṁ rūpā adhibhaṁsu, na bhikkhu rūpe adhibhosi;
root_text.sn35.243:5.13: saddā bhikkhuṁ adhibhaṁsu, na bhikkhu sadde adhibhosi;
root_text.sn35.243:5.14: gandhā bhikkhuṁ adhibhaṁsu, na bhikkhu gandhe adhibhosi;
root_text.sn35.243:5.15: rasā bhikkhuṁ adhibhaṁsu, na bhikkhu rase adhibhosi;
root_text.sn35.243:5.16: phoṭṭhabbā bhikkhuṁ adhibhaṁsu, na bhikkhu phoṭṭhabbe adhibhosi;
root_text.sn35.243:5.17: dhammā bhikkhuṁ adhibhaṁsu, na bhikkhu dhamme adhibhosi.
root_text.sn35.243:5.18: Ayaṁ vuccatāvuso, bhikkhu rūpādhibhūto, saddādhibhūto, gandhādhibhūto, rasādhibhūto, phoṭṭhabbādhibhūto, dhammādhibhūto, adhibhūto, anadhibhū, adhibhaṁsu naṁ pāpakā akusalā dhammā saṅkilesikā ponobhavikā sadarā dukkhavipākā āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇiyā.
root_text.sn35.243:6.2: Idhāvuso, bhikkhu cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā piyarūpe rūpe nādhimuccati, appiyarūpe rūpe na byāpajjati, upaṭṭhitakāyassati ca viharati appamāṇacetaso,
root_text.sn35.243:6.3: tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti yatthassa te uppannā pāpakā akusalā dhammā aparisesā nirujjhanti …pe…
root_text.sn35.243:6.5: manasā dhammaṁ viññāya piyarūpe dhamme nādhimuccati, appiyarūpe dhamme na byāpajjati, upaṭṭhitakāyassati ca viharati appamāṇacetaso,
root_text.sn35.243:6.6: tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti yatthassa te uppannā pāpakā akusalā dhammā aparisesā nirujjhanti.
root_text.sn35.243:6.7: Ayaṁ vuccatāvuso, bhikkhu anavassuto cakkhuviññeyyesu rūpesu …pe…
root_text.sn35.243:6.9: Evaṁvihāriñcāvuso, bhikkhuṁ cakkhuto cepi naṁ māro upasaṅkamati, neva labhati māro otāraṁ, na labhati māro ārammaṇaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.243:6.11: manato cepi naṁ māro upasaṅkamati, neva labhati māro otāraṁ, na labhati māro ārammaṇaṁ.
root_text.sn35.243:7.1: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, kūṭāgāraṁ vā sālā vā bahalamattikā addāvalepanā.
root_text.sn35.243:7.2: Puratthimāya cepi naṁ disāya puriso ādittāya tiṇukkāya upasaṅkameyya, neva labhetha aggi otāraṁ, na labhetha aggi ārammaṇaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.243:7.8: yato kutoci cepi naṁ puriso ādittāya tiṇukkāya upasaṅkameyya, neva labhetha aggi otāraṁ, na labhetha aggi ārammaṇaṁ.
root_text.sn35.243:7.9: Evameva kho, āvuso, evaṁvihāriṁ bhikkhuṁ cakkhuto cepi naṁ māro upasaṅkamati, neva labhati māro otāraṁ, na labhati māro ārammaṇaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.243:7.10: manato cepi naṁ māro upasaṅkamati, neva labhati māro otāraṁ, na labhati māro ārammaṇaṁ.
root_text.sn35.243:7.11: Evaṁvihārī cāvuso, bhikkhu rūpe adhibhosi, na rūpā bhikkhuṁ adhibhaṁsu;
root_text.sn35.243:7.12: sadde bhikkhu adhibhosi, na saddā bhikkhuṁ adhibhaṁsu;
root_text.sn35.243:7.13: gandhe bhikkhu adhibhosi, na gandhā bhikkhuṁ adhibhaṁsu;
root_text.sn35.243:7.14: rase bhikkhu adhibhosi, na rasā bhikkhuṁ adhibhaṁsu;
root_text.sn35.243:7.15: phoṭṭhabbe bhikkhu adhibhosi, na phoṭṭhabbā bhikkhuṁ adhibhaṁsu;
root_text.sn35.243:7.16: dhamme bhikkhu adhibhosi, na dhammā bhikkhuṁ adhibhaṁsu.
root_text.sn35.243:7.17: Ayaṁ vuccatāvuso, bhikkhu rūpādhibhū, saddādhibhū, gandhādhibhū, rasādhibhū, phoṭṭhabbādhibhū, dhammādhibhū, adhibhū, anadhibhūto, adhibhosi te pāpake akusale dhamme saṅkilesike ponobhavike sadare dukkhavipāke āyatiṁ jātijarāmaraṇiye.
root_text.sn35.243:8.1: Atha kho bhagavā uṭṭhahitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn35.243:8.3: Sādhu kho tvaṁ, moggallāna, bhikkhūnaṁ avassutapariyāyañca anavassutapariyāyañca abhāsī”ti.
root_text.sn35.243:9.3: Attamanā te bhikkhū āyasmato mahāmoggallānassa bhāsitaṁ abhinandunti.

translation_text.sn35.243:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Sakyans, near Kapilavatthu in the Banyan Tree Monastery.
translation_text.sn35.243:1.2: Now at that time a new town hall had recently been constructed for the Sakyans of Kapilavatthu. It had not yet been occupied by an ascetic or brahmin or any human at all.
translation_text.sn35.243:1.3: Then the Sakyans of Kapilavatthu went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.sn35.243:1.4: “Sir, a new town hall has recently been constructed for the Sakyans of Kapilavatthu. It has not yet been occupied by an ascetic or brahmin or any human at all.
translation_text.sn35.243:1.5: May the Buddha be the first to use it,
translation_text.sn35.243:1.6: and only then will the Sakyans of Kapilavatthu use it.
translation_text.sn35.243:1.7: That would be for the lasting welfare and happiness of the Sakyans of Kapilavatthu.”
translation_text.sn35.243:2.1: Knowing that the Buddha had consented, the Sakyans got up from their seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on their right. Then they went to the new town hall, where they spread carpets all over, prepared seats, set up a water jar, and placed an oil lamp. Then they went back to the Buddha and told him of their preparations, saying,
translation_text.sn35.243:2.4: Then the Buddha robed up and, taking his bowl and robe, went to the new town hall together with the Saṅgha of mendicants. Having washed his feet he entered the town hall and sat against the central column facing east.
translation_text.sn35.243:2.5: The Saṅgha of mendicants also washed their feet, entered the town hall, and sat against the west wall facing east, with the Buddha right in front of them.
translation_text.sn35.243:2.6: The Sakyans of Kapilavatthu also washed their feet, entered the town hall, and sat against the east wall facing west, with the Buddha right in front of them.
translation_text.sn35.243:2.7: The Buddha spent much of the night educating, encouraging, firing up, and inspiring the Sakyans with a Dhamma talk. Then he sent them off, saying,
translation_text.sn35.243:2.10: “Yes, sir,” replied the Sakyans. They got up from their seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on their right, before leaving.
translation_text.sn35.243:3.1: And then, soon after the Sakyans had left, the Buddha addressed Venerable Mahāmoggallāna,
translation_text.sn35.243:3.2: “Moggallāna, the Saṅgha of mendicants is rid of dullness and drowsiness.
translation_text.sn35.243:3.3: Give them some Dhamma talk as you feel inspired.
translation_text.sn35.243:3.7: And then the Buddha spread out his outer robe folded in four and laid down in the lion’s posture—on the right side, placing one foot on top of the other—mindful and aware, and focused on the time of getting up.
translation_text.sn35.243:3.8: There Venerable Mahāmoggallāna addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn35.243:3.12: “I will teach you the explanation of the festering and the not festering.
translation_text.sn35.243:3.13: Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.sn35.243:4.2: Take a mendicant who sees a sight with the eye. If it’s pleasant they hold on to it, but if it’s unpleasant they dislike it. They live with mindfulness of the body unestablished and their heart stunted.
translation_text.sn35.243:4.3: And they don’t truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where those arisen bad, unskillful qualities cease without anything left over.
translation_text.sn35.243:4.4: They hear a sound … smell an odor … taste a flavor … feel a touch …
translation_text.sn35.243:4.5: know an idea with the mind. If it’s pleasant they hold on to it, but if it’s unpleasant they dislike it. They live with mindfulness of the body unestablished and a limited heart.
translation_text.sn35.243:4.6: And they don’t truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where those arisen bad, unskillful qualities cease without anything left over.
translation_text.sn35.243:4.7: This is called a mendicant who is festering when it comes to sights known by the eye,
translation_text.sn35.243:4.10: When a mendicant lives like this, if Māra comes at them through the eye he finds a vulnerability and gets an opportunity.
translation_text.sn35.243:4.11: If Māra comes at them through the ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.243:4.12: mind he finds a vulnerability and gets an opportunity.
translation_text.sn35.243:5.1: Suppose there was a house made of reeds or straw that was dried up, withered, and decrepit.
translation_text.sn35.243:5.2: If a person came to it with a burning grass torch from the east,
translation_text.sn35.243:5.8: or from anywhere, the fire would find a vulnerability, it would get an opportunity.
translation_text.sn35.243:5.9: In the same way, when a mendicant lives like this, if Māra comes at them through the eye he finds a vulnerability and gets an opportunity.
translation_text.sn35.243:5.10: If Māra comes at them through the ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.243:5.11: mind he finds a vulnerability and gets an opportunity.
translation_text.sn35.243:5.12: When a mendicant lives like this, they’re mastered by sights,
translation_text.sn35.243:5.17: and ideas, they don’t master these things.
translation_text.sn35.243:5.18: This is called a mendicant who has been mastered by sights, sounds, smells, tastes, touches, and ideas. They’re mastered, not a master. Bad, unskillful qualities have mastered them, which are corrupting, leading to future lives, hurtful, and resulting in suffering and future rebirth, old age, and death.
translation_text.sn35.243:6.1: And how is someone not festering?
translation_text.sn35.243:6.2: Take a mendicant who sees a sight with the eye. If it’s pleasant they don’t hold on to it, and if it’s unpleasant they don’t dislike it. They live with mindfulness of the body established and a limitless heart.
translation_text.sn35.243:6.3: And they truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where those arisen bad, unskillful qualities cease without anything left over.
translation_text.sn35.243:6.4: They hear a sound … smell an odor … taste a flavor … feel a touch …
translation_text.sn35.243:6.5: know an idea with the mind. If it’s pleasant they don’t hold on to it, and if it’s unpleasant they don’t dislike it. They live with mindfulness of the body established and a limitless heart.
translation_text.sn35.243:6.6: And they truly understand the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where those arisen bad, unskillful qualities cease without anything left over.
translation_text.sn35.243:6.7: This is called a mendicant who is not festering when it comes to sights known by the eye,
translation_text.sn35.243:6.8: sounds … smells … tastes … touches … ideas known by the mind.
translation_text.sn35.243:6.9: When a mendicant lives like this, if Māra comes at them through the eye he doesn’t find a vulnerability or get an opportunity.
translation_text.sn35.243:6.10: If Māra comes at them through the ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.243:6.11: mind he doesn’t find a vulnerability or get an opportunity.
translation_text.sn35.243:7.1: Suppose there was a bungalow or hall made of thick clay with its plaster still wet.
translation_text.sn35.243:7.2: If a person came to it with a burning grass torch from the east,
translation_text.sn35.243:7.8: or from anywhere, the fire wouldn’t find a vulnerability, it would get no opportunity.
translation_text.sn35.243:7.9: In the same way, when a mendicant lives like this, if Māra comes at them through the eye he doesn’t find a vulnerability or get an opportunity.
translation_text.sn35.243:7.10: If Māra comes at them through the ear … nose … tongue … body … mind he doesn’t find a vulnerability or get an opportunity.
translation_text.sn35.243:7.11: When a mendicant lives like this, they master sights,
translation_text.sn35.243:7.16: and ideas, they’re not mastered by these things.
translation_text.sn35.243:7.17: This is called a mendicant who has mastered sights, sounds, smells, tastes, touches, and ideas. They’re a master, not mastered. Bad, unskillful qualities have been mastered by them, which are corrupting, leading to future lives, hurtful, and resulting in suffering and future rebirth, old age, and death.
translation_text.sn35.243:7.18: That’s how someone is not festering.”
translation_text.sn35.243:8.1: Then the Buddha got up and said to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna:
translation_text.sn35.243:8.3: It’s good that you’ve taught this explanation of the festering and the not festering.”
translation_text.sn35.243:9.1: This is what Venerable Mahāmoggallāna said,
translation_text.sn35.243:9.3: Satisfied, the mendicants approved what Mahāmoggallāna said.

variant_text.sn35.243:1.2: santhāgāraṁ → saṇṭhāgāraṁ (sya-all); sandhāgāraṁ (mr) | anajjhāvuṭṭhaṁ → anajjhāvutthaṁ (bj, sya-all, km)
variant_text.sn35.243:4.10: labhateva → labhatheva (sya1ed, sya2ed); labhetha (mr) | labhati → labhetha (sya2ed, mr)
variant_text.sn35.243:7.17: dhammādhibhū, adhibhū, anadhibhūto → anadhibhūto kehici kilesehi (mr)
